skill.name: google-maps-search-api-skill
skill.description: This skill is designed to help users automatically extract business data from Google Maps search results. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when the user makes the following requests searching for coffee shops in a specific city, finding dentists or medical clinics nearby, tracking competitors' locations in a certain area, extracting business leads from Google Maps lists, gathering restaurant data for market research, finding hotels or accommodation options in a region, locating specific services like coworking spaces or gyms, monitoring new business openings in a neighborhood, collecting contact information and addresses for sales prospecting, analyzing price ranges and cuisines of local eateries, getting ratings and review counts for a list of businesses, exporting local business data into a CRM or database.
skill.summary: Extract business data from Google Maps including names, addresses, ratings, reviews, and contact info via BrowserAct API integration.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 72 potential issues but manual review determined they are false positives. The skill uses proper credential handling (environment variables), makes authenticated API calls only to a documented endpoint (api.browseract.com), and contains no malicious patterns. The detected "dangerous combination" is intentional API integration, not malicious behavior. External command detections were triggered by markdown code fences in documentation, not actual shell execution.

high_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credentials
high_findings.description: Script reads API key from BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. This is proper credential handling, not a vulnerability.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 90 correctly uses os.getenv('BROWSERACT_API_KEY') following security best practices for credential management

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for workflow execution. This is intentional API integration for data extraction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate API integration to BrowserAct's documented endpoint for Google Maps data extraction

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Detection Misclassified as Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick characters in SKILL.md markdown documentation as Ruby shell commands. No actual shell execution exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown code fences (```) triggered false positive detection; no actual shell commands are executed

low_findings.title: Error Message Strings Misclassified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged literal strings like 'Invalid authorization' and 'Task Status' as reconnaissance patterns. These are legitimate error handling and logging outputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: String literals used for error message detection and progress logging, not actual reconnaissance activity

dangerous_patterns.title: Intentional API Integration Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Skill combines network access with credential handling to integrate with BrowserAct API. This is documented and intentional functionality, not malicious.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static analysis 'dangerous combination' heuristic triggered but actual code is legitimate API wrapper using environment variable credentials

content.user_title: Extract Business Data from Google Maps
content.value_statement: Finding business leads and contact information on Google Maps is time-consuming manual work. This skill automates Google Maps searches to extract structured business data including names, addresses, ratings, reviews, and contact info in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Search Google Maps by keyword, location, and language to find businesses
Extract business names, full addresses, ratings, and review counts
Retrieve price ranges, cuisine types, and amenity tags for businesses
Collect review snippets and service options for each result
Export structured data for CRM integration or spreadsheet analysis
Filter results by country and language for localized research
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication and execution
Limited to 100 results per search by default
Data quality depends on Google Maps information completeness

use_cases.title: Sales Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Extract contact information for businesses in your target market. Find all dental clinics in your city with addresses and ratings for prospecting campaigns.
use_cases.target_user: Sales representatives and business development teams

use_cases.title: Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Map competitor locations and analyze their ratings, price ranges, and customer reviews. Track competitor density in specific neighborhoods.

use_cases.title: Local Business Discovery
use_cases.description: Find specific service providers in a region. Locate 24-hour pharmacies, emergency plumbers, or coworking spaces for planning purposes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Business Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a simple list of businesses matching a keyword and location

prompt_templates.title: Localized Search with Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for Italian restaurants in London, UK, with ratings and review counts
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need country-specific results with quality indicators

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Starbucks locations in Chicago with their ratings and addresses for competitive analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when mapping competitor presence across a market

prompt_templates.title: Lead List Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract a list of real estate agencies in Sydney including names, addresses, ratings, and review counts for our sales team
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when building structured lead lists for CRM import

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running searches to avoid authentication errors
Specify country and language parameters to get relevant results for your target market
Use specific keywords like business categories or service types for more accurate results
anti_patterns: Do not use vague keywords like 'business' or 'shop' - be specific about the business type you need
Do not expect real-time data - Google Maps data is updated periodically, not continuously
Do not ignore API key errors - invalid or expired keys will prevent any search from running

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key from https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How many results can I get per search?
faq.answer: The default maximum is 100 results per search. You can adjust this with the max_dates parameter.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: Common languages include en, de, fr, it, es, ja, zh-CN, and zh-TW. The default is English.

faq.question: Can I search in any country?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify country codes like us, gb, ca, au, de, fr, jp to bias results toward that region.

faq.question: How long does a search take?
faq.answer: Execution typically takes several minutes due to automated browser operations. The script outputs status logs to show progress.

faq.question: What data fields are returned?
faq.answer: Results include business name, full address, rating, review count, price range, cuisine type, amenity tags, review snippets, and service options.
